feature: Gate Request — container initiates action approval
user_story: As a container runtime (OpenClaw agent), I want to request approval for a destructive action so that the user can explicitly approve or deny it before it is executed.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/internal/runtime/<tenant_id>/gate/request. Authenticated via X-Internal-Key + X-Tenant-Id headers (validated by validate_internal_runtime_request; returns 403 on failure). Body: {action_type, payload, display_summary}. Validates action_type against ActionType enum (returns 400 on unknown type) and requires non-empty display_summary (400 if missing). If tenant.model_tier == 'starter', returns 403 with status='blocked' and a store-safe message about prompt injection risk — this check precedes all other logic and cannot be bypassed even if gate_all_actions=False. If auto-approve is in effect (tenant.gate_all_actions=False AND tenant.gate_acknowledged_risk=True, OR a GatePreference.require_confirmation=False record exists), creates ActionAuditLog with result=approved and returns 200 {action_id: null, status: 'approved', auto_approved: true}. Otherwise creates PendingAction (status=pending, expires_at=now+5min), dispatches send_gate_confirmation to the tenant's preferred channel, and returns 202 {action_id, status: 'pending', expires_at}.
dependencies: apps/integrations/internal_auth (validate_internal_runtime_request); apps/tenants/models (Tenant, gate_all_actions, gate_acknowledged_risk, model_tier); apps/actions/messaging (send_gate_confirmation); Telegram Bot API or LINE Messaging API
inventory_notes: Starter-tier block is intentionally store-safe (no upgrade link) per App Store Guideline 3.1.1. Auto-approval path logs an ActionAuditLog even without creating a PendingAction. The action_type must be one of: gmail_trash, gmail_delete, gmail_send, calendar_delete, drive_delete, task_delete.

feature: Gate Poll — container polls for approval decision
user_story: As a container runtime (OpenClaw agent), I want to poll for whether the user has approved or denied a pending action so that I can proceed or abort accordingly.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/internal/runtime/<tenant_id>/gate/<action_id>/poll. Authenticated via X-Internal-Key + X-Tenant-Id headers (403 on failure). Resolves PendingAction by id + tenant_id (404 if not found). If action.is_expired (now > expires_at AND status == PENDING), transitions status to 'expired', creates ActionAuditLog with result=expired, and returns 200 {action_id, status: 'expired'}. Otherwise returns 200 {action_id, status} with the current status unchanged. The container polls in a loop until status is approved, denied, or expired.
inventory_notes: Expiry transition happens lazily on poll — there is no background sweeper that sets status=expired proactively (the Celery task in tasks.py does that as a belt-and-suspenders sweep). Both poll and the Celery sweeper emit an ActionAuditLog on expiry, so duplicate logs are possible for expired actions.

feature: Gate Respond — Django-internal callback from button press
user_story: As Django's own Telegram/LINE callback handler, I want to record the user's approve/deny button press so that the agent container can learn the decision via the poll endpoint.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/gate/<action_id>/respond/. Authenticated via X-Deploy-Secret header matched against DEPLOY_SECRET setting (403 if absent or wrong; 500 if DEPLOY_SECRET not configured). Body: {action: 'approve' | 'deny'} (400 on any other value). Resolves PendingAction by id (404 if not found). If action.status != PENDING returns 409 with current status. If action.is_expired, transitions to expired, creates ActionAuditLog, and returns 410. On valid response, writes action.status (approved/denied), action.responded_at=now, creates ActionAuditLog, then calls update_gate_message to edit/follow-up the platform confirmation message. Returns 200 {status}.
dependencies: DEPLOY_SECRET setting; apps/actions/messaging (update_gate_message); Telegram Bot API (editMessageText) or LINE Messaging API (push)
inventory_notes: This endpoint is called by the Telegram poller and LINE webhook handlers directly in-process, not via HTTP — but the view exists as a proper HTTP endpoint. The same logic is duplicated in-process in poller._handle_gate_callback and line_webhook._handle_gate_postback for Telegram and LINE respectively, bypassing this endpoint. The HTTP endpoint path is present in urls.py and respond_urls.py.

feature: Telegram inline button — user approves/denies via Telegram callback query
user_story: As a subscriber using Telegram, I want to tap Approve or Deny buttons in the confirmation message so that I can control whether the agent executes a destructive action.
expected_behaviour: The Telegram poller receives a callback_query with callback_data of 'gate_approve:<id>' or 'gate_deny:<id>'. The poller calls _handle_gate_callback, which resolves the PendingAction scoped to the tenant (raises not-found and answers callback with 'Action not found' on miss). If action.status != PENDING answers with 'Already <status>' and returns. If is_expired, transitions to expired, creates ActionAuditLog, calls update_gate_message, answers '⏰ Expired'. Otherwise sets status to approved/denied, sets responded_at, creates ActionAuditLog, calls update_gate_message (which calls Telegram editMessageText to remove the inline keyboard and show the result), answers the callback with '✅ Approved' or '❌ Denied'. No HTTP round-trip to GateRespondView.
dependencies: Telegram Bot API (answerCallbackQuery, editMessageText); apps/actions/models (PendingAction, ActionAuditLog); apps/pii/redactor (rehydrate_for_tenant)
inventory_notes: The poller handles this entirely in-process and does not call the GateRespondView HTTP endpoint. The PendingAction is scoped to both id AND tenant — a user cannot approve another tenant's action.

feature: LINE postback — user approves/denies via LINE Flex Message button
user_story: As a subscriber using LINE, I want to tap Approve or Deny buttons in the confirmation Flex Message so that I can control whether the agent executes a destructive action.
expected_behaviour: The LINE webhook receives a postback event with data 'gate_approve:<id>' or 'gate_deny:<id>'. The handler calls _handle_gate_postback, which resolves the PendingAction scoped to the tenant. Handles already-resolved (silent return) and expired (transitions, logs, calls update_gate_message which sends a follow-up push text since LINE does not support editing). On valid response sets status + responded_at, creates ActionAuditLog, calls update_gate_message. LINE cannot edit messages so a new push message is sent showing the result.
dependencies: LINE Messaging API (push); apps/actions/models (PendingAction, ActionAuditLog); apps/pii/redactor (rehydrate_for_tenant)
inventory_notes: LINE has no message-edit API so _edit_line_message sends a new push message as a follow-up. This differs from Telegram which edits in place.

feature: Confirmation message dispatch — send gate prompt to user's platform
user_story: As the action gating system, I want to send a formatted confirmation prompt with Approve/Deny buttons to the user's preferred messaging channel so that they can review and decide on the pending action.
expected_behaviour: send_gate_confirmation(tenant, action) reads tenant.user.preferred_channel (defaults to 'telegram'). For Telegram: calls Telegram sendMessage with MarkdownV2 text including the rehydrated display_summary (PII tokens expanded) and an inline keyboard with callback_data 'gate_approve:<id>' and 'gate_deny:<id>'. Falls back to plain-text sendMessage if MarkdownV2 fails. Stores the returned message_id in action.platform_message_id and 'telegram' in action.platform_channel. For LINE: sends a Flex Message bubble with postback buttons, stores 'line-push-<action_id>' as the message_id (LINE push API does not return a message ID). If no suitable sender is found for the channel, logs a warning and does nothing.
dependencies: Telegram Bot API (sendMessage); LINE Messaging API (push); apps/pii/redactor (rehydrate_for_tenant); TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, LINE_CHANNEL_ACCESS_TOKEN settings
inventory_notes: PII tokens in display_summary are rehydrated before display so users see real names/emails, not anonymised tokens. Telegram fallback to plain text silently swallows the first error and retries without parse_mode.

user_story: As the platform, I want to periodically expire pending actions that have passed their deadline so that the agent container can receive a definitive 'expired' status on the next poll and the user's platform message is updated.
expected_behaviour: expire_stale_pending_actions() queries all PendingAction records with status=PENDING and expires_at < now. For each: sets status=EXPIRED, creates ActionAuditLog with result=expired, calls update_gate_message to edit/follow-up the platform message. Returns a summary string 'Expired N actions'. Errors in update_gate_message are caught and logged per action but do not abort the sweep. Intended to run every 60 seconds via Celery Beat or QStash cron.
dependencies: Celery or QStash cron trigger; apps/actions/messaging (update_gate_message); apps/actions/models (PendingAction, ActionAuditLog)
inventory_notes: The task is decorated @shared_task but this codebase uses QStash not Celery Beat — it is unclear whether this task is actually registered with QStash or remains unused. The comment says 'Celery Beat (or QStash cron)'. Expiry also happens lazily on poll and on GateRespondView calls, so this sweep is belt-and-suspenders.

user_story: As an admin/operator, I want to configure whether individual action types require user confirmation or are auto-approved per tenant so that power users can skip the confirmation prompt for specific operations.
expected_behaviour: GatePreference model has unique_together(tenant, action_type). require_confirmation=True (default) means the action goes through the full confirmation flow. require_confirmation=False means GateRequestView returns 200 approved immediately and writes an ActionAuditLog. The master switch is on the Tenant model: if gate_all_actions=False AND gate_acknowledged_risk=True, ALL action types are auto-approved regardless of GatePreference. Managed only via Django admin (GatePreferenceAdmin). No subscriber-facing API endpoint exists.
inventory_notes: Starter-tier check in GateRequestView runs before _should_auto_approve, so even a tenant with gate_all_actions=False and gate_acknowledged_risk=True on the starter tier is blocked. GatePreference has no subscriber-facing API — changes require Django admin or direct DB.

user_story: As the platform, I want to block all destructive actions for subscribers on the Starter plan so that they are protected from prompt-injection attacks that could cause irreversible data loss.
expected_behaviour: On POST /api/v1/internal/runtime/<tenant_id>/gate/request, if tenant.model_tier == 'starter', GateRequestView returns 403 {status: 'blocked', tier: 'starter', message: STARTER_BLOCKED_MESSAGE} for every action_type without any further checks. This check is applied before auto-approve logic and before creating any PendingAction. The message text deliberately avoids mentioning upgrades or billing URLs to comply with App Store Guideline 3.1.1. The OpenClaw config generator still loads the nbhd-action-gate skill for starter tenants with Google integration, but the gate endpoint will always block them.
inventory_notes: Config generator at apps/orchestrator/config_generator.py:2178 loads nbhd-action-gate for all GWS tenants including starter. The starter block is enforced at the API level, not at config-load time. Starter tier also receives only read-only GWS skills (gws-shared, gws-gmail-triage, gws-calendar-agenda) and never gets gws-gmail, gws-drive, gws-calendar, or gws-tasks.

feature: Action audit log — permanent record of every gate decision
user_story: As an admin/operator, I want a permanent audit trail of every gated action and its outcome so that I can review the history of destructive operations taken or rejected by agents.
expected_behaviour: ActionAuditLog records are created in four code paths: (1) auto-approval in GateRequestView (result=approved, responded_at=now); (2) user approval/denial in GateRespondView or poller/_handle_gate_callback or line_webhook/_handle_gate_postback (result=approved/denied, responded_at=now); (3) expiry in GatePollView when poll detects expiry; (4) expiry sweep in expire_stale_pending_actions task. Fields: tenant, action_type, action_payload (full JSON), display_summary, result, responded_at (null for expired), created_at. Ordering is newest-first. No API endpoint; accessible only via Django admin (ActionAuditLogAdmin, read-only created_at/responded_at).
inventory_notes: Expiry can be logged by multiple code paths (poll lazy-expiry + Celery sweep), potentially creating duplicate ActionAuditLog entries for the same PendingAction. There is no dedup guard.

feature: OpenClaw config — gate skill and GWS skill tier gating
user_story: As the orchestrator config generator, I want to inject the nbhd-action-gate skill and GWS tool skills into OpenClaw container config (with tier-appropriate restrictions) so that agents have access to gated destructive tools only when appropriate.
expected_behaviour: generate_openclaw_config checks if the tenant has an active Google integration. If yes: adds gws-shared, gws-gmail-triage, gws-calendar-agenda skill paths to skills.load.extraDirs (read-only, all tiers); always also adds nbhd-action-gate; sets env vars NBHD_TENANT_ID and NBHD_API_BASE_URL for the gate script to call Django. Starter tier: receives only the read-only GWS skills above. Higher tiers in principle receive destructive skills (gws-gmail, gws-gmail-send, gws-calendar, gws-drive, gws-tasks) but this conditional is not present in config_generator.py lines 2148-2186 — those lines only add the read-only set for all tiers. Gate env vars are not set without Google integration.
dependencies: apps/integrations/models (Integration, provider=google); apps/tenants/models (Tenant.model_tier); OpenClaw container runtime (nbhd-action-gate skill)
inventory_notes: As of the audited code, ALL tenants with Google integration (including non-starter) receive only the read-only GWS skills — the destructive skills (gws-gmail, etc.) are not added anywhere in config_generator. The gate skill is always added alongside GWS skills but the gate API would block starter tier. NBHD_TENANT_ID and NBHD_API_BASE_URL are required for the gate script to make HTTP calls back to Django.
